stats_fallback.technical_delivery_2023: Fincantieri counted Brilliant Lady as technically delivered in 2023, but Virgin Voyages did not accept her for commercial service at that time.
stats_fallback.warm_lay_up: Feb 2024 – mid-2025 (~18 months) drifting between anchorages off Cyprus, Civitavecchia (Italy), and Málaga (Spain). Virgin's delay cascaded through the fleet — Valiant Lady's Winter 2024 Caribbean and Resilient Lady's Summer 2024 Greek Isles seasons were both pulled to backfill itineraries.
stats_fallback.commercial_debut: 5 September 2025 — 5-night NYC → King's Wharf, Bermuda (overnight in port)
stats_fallback.delay_length: 21 months from originally announced first voyage
stats_fallback.christened: No publicly named traditional christening godmother. Virgin Voyages does not hold formal christening ceremonies. Virgin did not publish ceremony details for her 5 September 2025 NYC debut. Distinctive feature: Janice Sung mermaid figurehead.
stats_fallback.cabins_note: 1,408 cabins. Per CruiseMapper: 78 suites, 1,030 balcony, 95 oceanview, 105 interior. European king beds, Peek-a-Boo showers, Yellow Leaf hammocks on balconies.
stats_fallback.builder: Fincantieri S.p.A., Sestri Ponente yard (Genoa, Italy) — hull yard number 6304. Hull sections fabricated at Castellammare di Stabia and towed to Sestri Ponente for assembly.
stats_fallback.panama_canal_note: Only Lady Class ship with an adapted frame (modified deck and lifeboat overhangs) that allows Panama Canal transit. Sisters Scarlet, Valiant, and Resilient cannot. This is what enables Brilliant Lady's West Coast and Alaska deployment.
stats_fallback.mermaid_figurehead: Designed by Toronto illustrator Janice Sung, blending Renaissance and Japanese/Chinese art influences (per Virgin Voyages press release)
stats_fallback.adults_only_note: Strictly 18+. No children's clubs, no kids' menus, no family staterooms, no waterslides.

related.class_notes: Lady Class. Brilliant Lady is the FOURTH and NEWEST of Virgin Voyages' four Lady Class ships, all built by Fincantieri at Sestri Ponente. Her sisters are Scarlet Lady (yard 6287, delivered 14 February 2020), Valiant Lady (yard 6288, delivered 1 July 2021), and Resilient Lady (yard 6289, delivered 7 December 2022). She is hardware-identical to her sisters on the dining side but differs critically in one way: her adapted frame (modified deck and lifeboat overhangs) is the only one in the class that allows Panama Canal transit. This is what enables her West Coast and Alaska deployment. She also has the class-exclusive Janice Sung mermaid figurehead.

deployment.current_2026_04_11: Mid-voyage off Baja California on a 5-day round-trip from Los Angeles (April 7–12, 2026), en route Ensenada. ETA Ensenada 11 April 2026 14:00 UTC at 6.8 kn per VesselFinder.
deployment.2026_04_06_la_arrival: 6 April 2026 — first Virgin Voyages ship to dock at the Port of Los Angeles, inaugurating Virgin's first-ever West Coast deployment.
deployment.2026_may_alaska: May 2026 — repositions to Seattle for Virgin Voyages' inaugural Alaska season, also calling Vancouver.
deployment_history: 5 September 2025: Commercial debut 5-night maiden voyage NYC → King's Wharf, Bermuda (overnight)
6 April 2026 – May 2026: Virgin's first West Coast deployment from Los Angeles (Mexican Riviera, 5-day round-trips)
May 2026 onwards: Seattle (Virgin's first Alaska season, Inside Passage, Vancouver calls)

history.contract_signed: 31 October 2018 — ordered from Fincantieri for approximately EUR 700 million
history.hull_launched_castellammare: 24 February 2022 — hull sections launched at Castellammare di Stabia
history.float_out: 25 November 2022 — floated out at Sestri Ponente
history.technical_delivery_2023: 2023 — Fincantieri counted technical delivery for its own accounting; Virgin did not put her into service
history.2023_09_cancellation: September 2023 — Virgin Voyages cancelled all planned Brilliant Lady cruises, citing 'unexpected construction, supply chain, and staffing challenges' while completing a new round of financing. Cascading cancellations forced Virgin to pull Valiant Lady's Winter 2024 Caribbean and Resilient Lady's Summer 2024 Greek Isles seasons to backfill itineraries.
history.2024_02_formal_handover: February 2024 — formal Virgin handover at Fincantieri Palermo
history.warm_lay_up_18_months: February 2024 – mid-2025 — warm lay-up drifting between anchorages off Cyprus, Civitavecchia, and Málaga
history.2025_07_drydock: 20 July – 1 August 2025 — pre-launch drydock at Fincantieri Palermo
history.2025_09_05_commercial_debut: 5 September 2025 — commercial debut 5-night maiden voyage from New York to King's Wharf, Bermuda (with overnight in port). 21 months after her originally announced first voyage.
history.2026_04_06_la_arrival: 6 April 2026 — first Virgin Voyages ship to dock at the Port of Los Angeles, inaugurating Virgin's first-ever West Coast deployment

discrepancies_flagged.imo_verified_triple_source: IMO 9870654 verified across four independent sources (VesselFinder, CruiseMapper, MarineTraffic, MyShipTracking). Cross-checked against sisters Scarlet (9804801), Valiant (9805336), and Resilient (9805348) — all four distinct. NOTE: Brilliant's IMO sits in a different block (98706xx) from the three older sisters (98048xx/98053xx) because her keel was laid later and she was ordered October 2018 vs the others' earlier contracts. One agent earlier reported 9870656 as a 1-digit error; authoritative is 9870654.
discrepancies_flagged.delivery_framing_corrected: Brilliant Lady's 'delivery' is a three-phase story: (1) Fincantieri technical delivery 2023, (2) Virgin formal handover February 2024, (3) Commercial service 5 September 2025 after 18-month lay-up. Previous page framing of 'delivered 2020' or 'scheduled for 2023/2024' was wrong. Corrected throughout.
discrepancies_flagged.class_name_wrong: Previous stats JSON said 'Virgin Class'. Fixed to 'Lady Class'.
discrepancies_flagged.title_leak: Previous page had 'Brilliant Lady — Virgin Voyages | In the Wake' as ship name. Fixed.
discrepancies_flagged.main_dining_room_hallucination: Previous FAQ said Brilliant Lady offers 'the main dining room and buffet'. Virgin has neither.
discrepancies_flagged.wrong_dining_hero_image: Removed Cordelia Empress Food Court image.
discrepancies_flagged.no_godmother_invention: Explicitly states no publicly named godmother; Janice Sung mermaid figurehead noted as the distinctive naming detail.
discrepancies_flagged.no_cirque: Virgin Voyages has never had Cirque du Soleil.
discrepancies_flagged.delay_saga_documented: Previous page did not capture the 21-month delivery-delay saga. Now documented: ordered Oct 2018 → floated out Nov 2022 → technical delivery 2023 → Virgin cancelled cruises Sept 2023 → formal handover Feb 2024 → 18-month lay-up off Cyprus/Civitavecchia/Málaga → Palermo drydock Jul–Aug 2025 → commercial debut 5 Sept 2025 NYC. Also captured cascading cancellations that pulled Valiant Winter 2024 Caribbean and Resilient Summer 2024 Greek Isles seasons.
discrepancies_flagged.panama_canal_unique_documented: Added Brilliant-unique distinguishing feature: ONLY Lady Class ship with adapted frame allowing Panama Canal transit. This is what makes her West Coast and Alaska deployment possible.
discrepancies_flagged.first_la_arrival_documented: 6 April 2026 — first Virgin Voyages ship ever to dock at the Port of Los Angeles, inaugurating Virgin's first-ever West Coast deployment.
